prs.title: refactor: improved accessibility and visual consistency for shared UI
feature: added enrich search experience with browse grid and empty states
prs.body:
Implemented a more polished Search page experience with stronger empty and no-results states.

Summary:

- Added a Browse all category grid for the empty-query state using browseCategories mock data.
- Uses category colors for visually distinct browse tiles.
- Browse tiles now toggle category filtering and show local match counts.
- Improved no-results messaging with contextual text and a reset search action.
- Refined hover, active, and focus states for chips, browse tiles, input, and actions.
- Kept changes scoped to SearchPage.jsx and SearchPage.css.

prs.title: feature: implemented search page with browse chips and filtered results
Shared UI components
prs.body: added reusable media card, row, and section header components

prs.title: style: add global tokens, reset, and app utility styles
feat: unified Monte Carlo engine + blended benchmark comparison
prs.body:
Backend:
- Unified MC engine: single shared R matrix for metrics & chart data
- Log returns (np.log1p) for μ/Σ estimation (proper GBM)
- Rank-averaged named path selection across all T days
- SE + theoretical cross-check with Jensen's convexity adjustment
- Consistency assertion: |E[X]_metrics - E[X]_chart| < 2×SE
- Blended benchmark support: build_blended_benchmark_returns()
- compare_performance(): cumulative returns, TE, IR, Sharpe, classification
- API v3.0.0: optional benchmark_ticker + blended_benchmark JSON
- Pydantic v2 validation for blended weights (sum-to-1, non-negative)

Frontend:
- BenchmarkComparison.jsx: cumulative return bars, performance badges, metrics table
- UploadPortfolio.jsx: blended benchmark toggle (ON by default) with ticker/weight inputs
- Dashboard.jsx: wired BenchmarkComparison into layout
- RiskMetrics.jsx: replaced Sharpe Ratio with Historical VaR
- MonteCarloChart.jsx: fixed fallback GBM math

prs.title: feat: setup frontend with Vite + React + Tailwind v4 dashboard
carousel: remove ARIA attributes added by Slick to avoid duplicate screen-reader announcements

labels.name: Needs: Response
labels.description: Issues which require feedback from lead

prs.body:
closes #11354

### 🧭 Overview

This pull request removes unnecessary ARIA attributes that Slick Carousel adds automatically (such as `role="option"`, `aria-selected`, and `tabindex`). These attributes were causing screen readers to read out carousel items twice or label them as selectable, which was confusing for users relying on assistive technologies.

### ✅ What’s Changed

* Disabled Slick’s built-in accessibility option (`accessibility: false`) to stop it from injecting unwanted ARIA attributes.
* Added a lightweight keyboard handler to preserve arrow-key navigation (left/right) for keyboard users.
* Updated unit tests (`tests/unit/js/carousel.test.js`) to verify that the unwanted attributes are removed during initialization.

### 💡 Why It Matters

This fix improves the screen reader experience by preventing duplicate or misleading announcements, while keeping full keyboard navigation intact. There are **no visual or behavioral changes** for sighted users.

### 🧪 Testing

* Verified with unit tests that ARIA attributes are stripped correctly.
* Manually checked that arrow navigation still works as expected.

prs.title: fix: Privacy settings radio labels not clickable
prs.body:
### Problem
The radio button labels on the Privacy & Content Moderation Settings page were not functional - clicking them did not select their associated radio buttons.

### Fix
Updated the label `for` attributes to match their corresponding radio input `id` values:
- Changed `for="u0"` to `for="r0"` for public readlog Yes
- Changed `for="u1"` to `for="r1"` for public readlog No
- Changed `for="u0"` to `for="r2"` for safe mode Yes
- Changed `for="u1"` to `for="r3"` for safe mode No

### Testing
- Verified all label `for` attributes match their radio input `id`s
- Confirmed no other instances of the old `u0/u1` IDs remain
- Changes are minimal and focused on the specific accessibility issue

### References
- Related to #11382

prs.title: Added a CSS Robot
prs.body:
## Description
Added a friendly robot character created entirely with HTML and CSS.

## Features
- Pure CSS design with no animations or JavaScript
- Beginner-friendly code using basic shapes (rectangles, circles)
- Responsive centered layout
- Blue color scheme with gradient background

## Components
- Antenna with red ball
- Head with eyes and smile
- Body with control panel buttons
- Arms and legs with rounded edges
![Robot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bebcf1be-8440-4a17-895b-c6e2a665e9f3)

prs.title: Apple Sillicon install Guide

labels.name: beginner-friendly
labels.description: Easy to start, perfect for beginners

prs.body:
Documentation
Added a dedicated Apple Silicon (M1/M2+) installation guide with step-by-step instructions, system requirements, pre-install prep, recovery/security notes, media creation, installation flow, and post-install checks.
Included troubleshooting, FAQ, screenshot guidance, contribution notes, and a planned video tutorial outline.
Updated main project README to highlight and link the new Apple Silicon installation guide.

# PrismOS Installation on Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M-series)

This guide walks you through installing PrismOS on Apple Silicon Macs (M1, M2, and later). It's written for beginners and focuses on minimizing data loss and enabling an easy rollback.

## What you'll find in this guide

- System requirements
- Step-by-step installation (preparation, flashing, booting, first-run)
- Screenshots (placeholders) and how to capture them
- Troubleshooting
- FAQ
- Video tutorial plan and recording script

prs.title: Add: AniruddhaDwivedi
prs.body: 🪩 PR Title ✨ Added Modern Glassmorphism Login Template — “Nova Auth” ⸻ 🧠 Overview This PR introduces a fresh, futuristic login page built with HTML, CSS, and JS, designed exclusively for the open-source login templates collection. It’s a minimal yet immersive design that blends glassmorphism, depth blur, and gradient accents, offering a refined modern aesthetic for user authentication screens. ⸻ 💡 Highlights • 🎨 Glassmorphism Design — Semi-transparent panels with smooth blur and light diffusion • 🌃 Dark Mode Ready — Clean and contrast-balanced for night-friendly viewing • ⚡ Responsive Layout — Scales elegantly across all devices (mobile to desktop) • 🧩 Modular Code Structure — HTML, CSS, and JS kept in separate files for easy maintenance • 💫 Subtle Animations — Smooth hover and focus transitions inspired by Apple’s motion design ⸻ 🖼️ Visual Preview ⸻ 🏗️ Tech Stack • HTML5 — Semantic, accessible markup • CSS3 — Gradient overlays, glass blur, and transition effects • JavaScript — Simple form validation + interactive UI response ⸻ 🤝 Why It’s Unique Unlike standard login pages, Nova Auth embodies a cinematic glass aesthetic inspired by modern macOS and visionOS interfaces. It’s not just functional — it’s designed to feel alive with visual depth, elegance, and simplicity. ⸻ 🏷️ Hacktoberfest Note This PR is part of my Hacktoberfest 2025 contributions 🌐 Let’s make open-source interfaces beautiful together 💜
